commit.message:
fix: eliminate null transient state in DeleteGroup concurrent reads (#290)

## Problem

CI failure:
`WsBridgeIntegrationTests.Organization_DeleteGroup_RemovesFromServer`

```
System.NullReferenceException : Object reference not set to an instance of an object.
at WsBridgeIntegrationTests.<Organization_DeleteGroup_RemovesFromServer>b__2(SessionGroup g)
   in WsBridgeIntegrationTests.cs:line 520
```

**Root cause:** `List<T>.RemoveAll()` in .NET nulls out trailing array
elements via `Array.Clear` *before* decrementing `_size`. This creates a
transient window where:
- A background thread (WebSocket receive) is running `DeleteGroup →
RemoveAll`
- A concurrent reader (test polling thread, or the Blazor render thread)
iterates `Organization.Groups`
- The reader sees a null `SessionGroup` at an index that's still within
the old `_size`
- `g.Id` throws `NullReferenceException`

This only manifests on CI because thread scheduling variance is higher
in the GitHub Actions runner environment. It passes locally where the
polling loop rarely lands in the narrow null-transient window.

## Fix

Replace in-place `RemoveAll` with an **atomic list-reference
replacement**:

```csharp
// Before (produces transient null state):
Organization.Groups.RemoveAll(g => g.Id == groupId);

// After (atomic: readers see old complete list OR new list, never null elements):
Organization.Groups = Organization.Groups.Where(g => g.Id != groupId).ToList();
```

Assigning a new `List<T>` reference is atomic on 64-bit .NET (object
reference assignment). Readers that already hold a reference to the old
list continue to see all elements. Readers that pick up the new
reference see the post-delete state. No reader ever sees a null element.

## Testing

- ✅ `Organization_DeleteGroup_RemovesFromServer` passes
- ✅ All 50 `WsBridgeIntegrationTests` pass  
- ✅ 2026/2039 tests pass (13 pre-existing `PopupThemeTests` failures
unrelated to this change)

Fixes:
https://github.com/PureWeen/PolyPilot/actions/runs/22735831218/job/65936842712

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: add CI formatting learning to Dallas history

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(upload): don't abort on Python3 504; let Terraform confirm image state

When nginx returns a 504 on the upload response path, the data has already
been received and terraform_data's curl upload will confirm HTTP 200. Killing
Terraform on a Python3 error was incorrect — Terraform is the source of truth.

New flow: if the shell-side upload exits non-zero, log a warning and continue
waiting for Terraform. If Terraform exits 0 (image Active), the overall
operation succeeds. If Terraform also fails, the full log is printed and the
script exits 1 as before.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: computed-goto interpreter dispatch (closes #312) (#378)

Replace the central `match instr.opcode { ... }` block in the interpreter
with a static function-pointer dispatch table (`DISPATCH_TABLE`) for better
branch prediction. Each opcode maps to a dedicated handler function, giving
the CPU's branch predictor a direct-call target per opcode.

- Add `dispatch.rs`: DispatchAction enum, DispatchContext struct,
  OpcodeHandler type, 169 handler functions, static dispatch table
- Replace ~3400-line match block with 14-line table-dispatch loop
- Make helper functions and ACTIVE_DEBUGGER `pub(super)` for dispatch module
- Add event_loop module with TaskQueue, TimerHandle, EmbedderCallbacks
- Add stator_event_loop_* FFI functions for C++ embedder access

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix: keep cluster absent from descriptor server list when peer sends data for it (#3353)

* Fix: keep cluster absent from descriptor server list when peer sends data for it

## Problem

During a subscription (or re-subscription), two things can happen in the same
interaction for an endpoint whose device has a buggy descriptor:

1. The descriptor's ServerList is processed.  If a cluster is currently
   supported but missing from the ServerList, `pendingDelete = true` is set on
   it and a `rebuild` is scheduled.
2. Attribute data for that same cluster arrives in the same ReportData stream.
   `#synchronizeCluster` sets `pendingBehavior` on the cluster.

Depending on which chunk arrives first, one of the following would occur:

- **Descriptor first, cluster data second** (`pendingDelete` set, then
  `pendingBehavior` set): `#rebuild()` saw `pendingDelete` and called `drop()`,
  then hit `continue` — `pendingBehavior` was silently ignored. Cluster removed.
- **Cluster data first, descriptor second** (`pendingBehavior` set, then
  `pendingDelete` set): same outcome — `#rebuild()` dropped the cluster.

The net result: a cluster the device actively reports data for gets removed on
every re-subscription, and an INFO log appeared:
  `Removing server-X.@Y:Z.epN.<clusterName> from active endpoint`

A TODO comment already acknowledged this gap:
  `// TODO Should we somehow validate that against descriptor serverList`
  `// because we might load data not in there`

## Fix

Guard both write sites in the data-processing flow so they cancel each other:

1. **When `pendingBehavior` is set** (cluster data received): if `pendingDelete`
   is already set on the cluster, clear it — the pending deletion is cancelled.

2. **When `pendingDelete` would be set** (cluster absent from ServerList): if
   `pendingBehavior` is already set on the cluster, skip setting `pendingDelete`
   — the deletion is skipped entirely.

Together these two guards cover both arrival orderings.  Clusters that are
genuinely removed (absent from ServerList *and* no data received) are
unaffected: `pendingBehavior` is undefined, so `pendingDelete = true` is set
as before and `#rebuild()` drops them normally.

A `warn` log is emitted when the device inconsistency is detected so it remains
visible in logs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update packages/node/src/node/client/ClientStructure.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR review: add missing warn log and use hex.fixed for cluster id

- Emit the same warn log in #synchronizeCluster when pendingDelete is
  cleared (covers the "Descriptor first" arrival order; previously only
  the "cluster data first" path logged)
- Use hex.fixed(id, 8) instead of id.toString(16).padStart(8, "0") to
  match codebase convention
- Fix spelling: "Schrödinger's cluster"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Only schedule rebuild when a cluster is actually marked for deletion

If all unsupported clusters are kept due to receiving attribute data
(Schrödinger's cluster), no rebuild is needed and scheduling one
unnecessarily was wasteful.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* finalize

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(ai): Implement PPO agent, TimeGAN, VAE, FinGPT sentiment

P1.1: PPO Trading Agent (TorchSharp Actor-Critic)
- PPOModels.cs: Config, RolloutBuffer with GAE, training/action result DTOs
- PPOAgentService.cs: Sharpe-ratio reward, clipped surrogate loss, entropy bonus
- Actor-Critic networks with LayerNorm (69->256->128->3/1)

P1.2: TimeGAN Synthetic Data Service
- TimeGanModels.cs: Config, generation request/response, scenario types
- TimeGanService.cs: HTTP client for Python sidecar, data augmentation, validation

P1.3: Variational Autoencoder (LSTM-VAE)
- VAEModels.cs: Config, VaeAnalysisResult, training result DTOs
- VariationalAutoencoderService.cs: Anomaly detection via reconstruction error
- LSTM encoder/decoder, reparameterization trick, beta-VAE loss
- 4 latent risk factors extracted as TradeFeatureData features

P1.4: FinGPT/FinBERT Financial Sentiment
- FinGptSentiment.cs: Result DTOs, FinGptConfig (sidecar + Azure OpenAI)
- FinancialLlmSentimentService.cs: Dual-mode (sidecar/Azure OpenAI)
- ConcurrentDictionary caching (15min TTL), request throttling

4.3 Multi-Cloud K8s Orchestration:
- Create Kustomize base manifests (namespace, rbac, configmap, secrets, silo, web, redis, monitor)
- Create Azure overlay (multi-silo, AzureTable storage, GPU preference)
- Create DigitalOcean overlay (single silo, memory storage, cost-optimized)

refactor: reduce complexity of populateDynamicTags and loadPath

- Split populateDynamicTags (loader/loader.go) into 4 helper functions:
  * populateSymbolTags - handles DT_SYMTAB, DT_STRTAB, DT_HASH, DT_GNU_HASH
  * populateRelocationTags - handles DT_RELA, DT_RELASZ, DT_RELAENT, DT_JMPREL, DT_PLTRELSZ
  * populateInitFiniTags - handles DT_INIT, DT_FINI, init/fini arrays
  * populateSoname - handles DT_SONAME
  Cyclomatic complexity: 17 → 1 (94.1% improvement)

- Refactor loadPath (dl/dl.go) to extract 5 helper functions:
  * incrementRefIfLoaded - handles cycle detection ref counting
  * checkLoadingCache - coordinates concurrent loading
  * releaseLoadingSlot - cleanup on error paths
  * loadDependencies - recursive dependency loading
  * registerLibrary - global cache registration
  Cyclomatic complexity: 14 → 6 (57.1% improvement)

Resolves AUDIT.md MEDIUM-01. All tests pass, zero regressions.

commit.message:
Allow hyphens in function names

Hyphens are now valid in Runfile function names (non-leading), matching
the convention of make, just, and npm scripts.

- Grammar: add '-' to identifier character set
- Transpiler: sanitise_name() now replaces '-' with '_' (bash does not
  allow hyphens in function names, so my-func becomes my_func in the
  generated script)
- MCP: tool names keep hyphens as-is (the MCP spec allows [a-zA-Z0-9_-])
- Parser: remove now-redundant hyphen hint from friendly_hint() and
  update identifier hint text to mention '-'
- Tests: add integration tests for inline and block hyphenated functions;
  update test_sanitise_name; remove Runfile stray my-func line

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(upload): skip terraform_data curl upload if image already Active

When harvester-vm.sh's Python3 uploader completes before terraform_data
reaches its curl upload, the server closes the connection with a
PROTOCOL_ERROR (the upload action is no longer available on an Active image).

Add a final Active-state check right before the curl upload so that if an
external upload already succeeded the terraform_data provisioner exits 0
instead of attempting a redundant upload.

commit.message:
fix(backend): server starts immediately, DB retries in background

Previously: server only started after connectDB() succeeded — so any
DB hiccup (Supabase paused, network, bad creds) crashed the whole
process and Render would restart-loop endlessly.

Now:
- server.listen() fires immediately on startup
- connectDB() runs non-blocking with exponential backoff (5s→60s)
- /api/health responds even while DB is reconnecting
- Once Supabase is reachable the pool heals automatically

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(watchdog): import _run_workflow_background from correct module

Wrong import caused watchdog to fail when relaunching stuck AC cycles.

commit.message:
Add exponential backoff recovery to PollingAgent

Replace single-attempt session recovery with 5-attempt exponential
backoff (2s → 4s → 8s → 16s → 30s cap), matching the TUI
SessionManager pattern.

- RecoverSessionWithBackoffAsync: new method with retry loop
- RunLoopAsync: tracks consecutive failures, adaptive polling
  interval (normal × 2^failures, capped at 5 min), 60s cooldown
  after total recovery failure
- SendMessageAsync: catches communication errors, recovers with
  backoff, and retries the message once

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(backend): robust URL parser — split on last @ to handle password with @

pg-connection-string uses Node's url.parse which splits on the FIRST @,
mangling passwords that contain @ (encoded or raw).

Replace connectionString Pool config with a manual parser that:
- Finds the LAST @ as the user/host boundary
- Calls decodeURIComponent on the password (handles both raw @ and %40)
- Passes individual params to pg.Pool, bypassing url.parse entirely

Works with both Render dashboard URL (raw @) and .env URL (%40).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Remove locale:missing; standardise on locale:download:missing (#8204)

The local-diff approach (`locale-build-missing.js` / `locale:missing`)
was redundant — POEditor's own `filters=untranslated` export API already
computes missing terms server-side with no prior full download required.
This removes the old script and wires everything to
`locale:download:missing`.

## Changes

- **Deleted** `locale/scripts/locale-build-missing.js`
- **`package.json`** — removed `locale:missing` script
- **`locale/scripts/locale-translate.js`** — updated fallback hint to
`locale:download:missing`
- **`locale/scripts/poeditor-missing-downloader.js`** — cleaned up JSDoc
(dropped cross-references to deleted file)
- **`locale/README.md`** — collapsed two-option workflow to single
canonical approach; removed `locale:missing` from scripts table and
quick-reference block
- **`.github/workflows/locale-poeditor-download.yml`** — replaced `npm
run locale:missing` step with `npm run locale:download:missing`; updated
commit step paths from `locale/missing-terms/poeditor-*.json` →
`locale/terms/missing/`
- **`.agents/skills/churchcrm/locale-ai-translation.md`** and
**`.claude/commands/locale-release.md`** — Quick Start and scripts table
updated to `locale:download:missing`

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT TIPS -->
---

💡 You can make Copilot smarter by setting up custom instructions,
customizing its development environment and configuring Model Context
Protocol (MCP) servers. Learn more [Copilot coding agent
tips](https://gh.io/copilot-coding-agent-tips) in the docs.

---------

repository.name: CRM
repository.description: ChurchCRM - A free and open-source Church Management Software (ChMS) to help churches manage their membership data, groups, events, and finances.

commit.message:
fix(shared,docker): resolve 4 LOW audit issues (L-09,L-12,L-14,L-15)

- L-09: Add indexnow-net custom bridge network to all Docker services
- L-12: Add in-memory TTL cache (1h, max 10k entries) for IP geolocation
- L-14: Replace `as RegistrationCountry[]` cast with type-guard filter
- L-15: REGEX_PATTERNS now delegates to VALIDATION_PATTERNS (no duplication)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(upload): fix IMAGE_SIZE bash syntax error in terraform_data heredoc

$$(wc...) expands $$ to the shell PID then makes (wc...) an unexpected
token — syntax error. Terraform only interpolates ${...}, not $(...),
so a plain $(wc...) passes through to bash as valid command substitution.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[identity] Terms Acceptance Requirement fix. Ensure user claims are loaded before terms check (#945)

* [identity] Ensure user claims are loaded before terms check

Load user claims if missing before validating terms acceptance.
Update logic to require acceptance only if terms were modified
in the past and user's acceptance predates the modification.

* Discard unused result from GetClaimsAsync call

Previously, the result of GetClaimsAsync was assigned to a variable that was never used. Now, the result is explicitly discarded, clarifying that the call is made only for its side effects. No functional changes were made.

* Apply suggestion from @Copilot

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve 7 low-priority audit issues (L-17 through L-23)

- L-17: Replace Object type with Record<string, unknown> in Resilient
  decorator, remove eslint-disable comments
- L-18: Replace array.shift() with O(1) circular buffer in
  CircuitBreaker failure tracking
- L-19: Use Date getMonth/getFullYear for precise month calculation
  in formatTimeAgo instead of 30-day approximation
- L-20: Replace dynamic grid-cols-${n} with static lookup map in
  StatsCardsSkeleton for Tailwind purge safety
- L-21: Add console.warn on clipboard write failure in ErrorState
- L-22: Move 'use client' to first line in useActivityLogger and
  useAdminActivityLogger hooks
- L-23: Add @indexnow/shared to externals in supabase-client tsup config

commit.message:
Show requesting agent ID in permission prompts

Add agentId parameter to the OnAskUser callback so the actual
requesting agent is passed through from the SessionFactory hook
rather than relying on the TUI's active agent state.

PermissionRenderable now renders the agent name in the border:
  🔐 email-watcher → bash

Both OpenTUI and legacy TUI implementations are updated.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
